title: AI Local Advisory Demo
subtitle: First vertical slice for AI-enhanced living-doc exports
scope: Show how a rendered living doc can expose document-grounded local AI advisory slots for the first three convergence types.
objective: Demonstrate a standalone living-doc export that can run local advisory tasks over its own typed document content without mutating the canonical source.
successCondition: The rendered HTML exposes section-level local AI advisory cards, embeds a typed AI spec, and keeps normal document rendering intact when no local model is used.

invariants.name: Document grounded only
invariants.statement: Every advisory result must derive from the document payload embedded in the export.

invariants.name: Standalone HTML preserved
invariants.statement: The exported living doc must remain fully standalone and readable even if no local model endpoint is available.

invariants.name: Read-only advisory
invariants.statement: The advisory layer must not change canonical document content or replace Cmd-K editing.

callout.title: Intent
items: Use one exported living doc to show the advisory layer in context.
Keep the feature additive to the current renderer rather than creating a second authoring flow.

data.name: Renderer enhancement mode

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: The export now knows how to emit advisory regions and an embedded AI spec.

data.name: AI Render Graph browser runtime

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: The export now embeds the real ai-render-graph browser runtime from the local vendor repo while staying a standalone HTML artifact.

data.name: Type-driven advisory mapping

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: Prompt construction and output schemas are grounded in convergence type semantics rather than ad hoc per-document rules.

callout.title: Boundary
items: This first pass is document-grounded only.
The export does not assume internet search, repo crawling, or other external tools.

data.name: Contract-level render proof
data.currentCoverage: Contract tests verify that AI advisory UI, embedded spec, separate advisory metadata, and expected task bindings appear in the rendered HTML.
data.nextStep: Keep the ai-render-graph spec contract stable as more convergence types and profiles are added.
gaps: Contract tests do not exercise a real local model response yet.

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: This proves the export shape, not model quality.

data.name: Browser-level rendered proof
data.currentCoverage: A Playwright test loads the rendered HTML and checks that section advisory blocks and task bindings are visible.
data.nextStep: Broaden browser checks as more task types and UI affordances are introduced.
gaps: No visual regression layer exists yet for advisory-card presentation.

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: This confirms the exported document can show the advisory surface in a browser.

data.name: Document-only grounding boundary
data.currentCoverage: Prompts include document root context, section context, type guidance, and normalized items only.
data.nextStep: Decide later whether to layer optional tool-backed enrichment on top of this base mode.
gaps: No explicit search-enabled mode exists yet.

ticketIds.title: Add AI Render Graph based living-doc export enhancement

notes: The current scope stays intentionally narrow so local models like Gemma can be useful from the export alone.

callout.title: Current proof posture
items: The system can now render type-aware advisory slots inside an exported living doc.
The remaining proof gap is real task hydration with stable mocked and live local-model coverage.

data.name: Embedded export shape exists
notes: The export now includes a typed advisory spec and section task bindings.

data.name: Advisory UI appears in a rendered doc
notes: The advisory cards are visible in a real rendered HTML document with section-level run controls.

data.name: Hydrated advisory outputs from model calls
notes: The ai-render-graph runtime now makes real local-model calls from the exported HTML, and browser coverage includes mocked hydration proof.

data.name: Library-native source-bound task inputs
notes: Task input now binds through ai-render-graph source collection from rendered document sections instead of relying only on prepacked constant payloads.
